metric_value: NT Server 4.0 being overused for enterprise-scale implementations it cannot support; NOS use mostly workgroup

metric_value: NT 5.0 is a 1999 production issue — no relief coming in 1998; buyers must sort out 1998 on their own

metric_name: IBM OS/2 Warp Server market position
metric_value: IBM not anticipating significant market share gains; maintaining installed base; $30B IBM revenue relies on OS/2
notes: IBM challenge is to stem mindshare erosion from Microsoft NT marketing.

metric_value: OS/2 Warp Server excellent for file/print; exceeds NT Server functionality even with NT extra-cost utilities
notes: Aberdeen conclusion from supplier abstract section.

metric_value: NT Server has almost non-existent security at Windows desktop level vs. very adequate Unix and IBM/Novell NOS
notes: Security spectrum: mainframe > Unix > IBM/Novell NOS >> NT Server.

metric_value: Battle between Novell NDS (shipping) and Microsoft Active Directory (upcoming) is truly strategic
notes: Central enterprise directory is key NOS battleground.

metric_value: Internet INCREASES need for powerful NOSs — directory services
notes: expert-opinion

metric_value: Sales of all three major NOS vendors increased over past two years as market expands and orgs upgrade/consolidate
notes: Blurring of OS and NOS lines benefits all vendors.

metric_value: Vast majority of NT Server NOS sales are in workgroup; few network managers seriously consider it as enterprise NOS
notes: NT wins as workgroup server; not enterprise NOS.

metric_value: Three forces driving NOS re-examination: 1) LAN linking into WANs; 2) M&A creating multi-NOS environments; 3) concentration in Microsoft/IBM/Novell/Unix
notes: Key finding in Summary of Findings chapter.

metric_value: NT 4.0 compliant with minor issues; requires Service Pack 3 plus Site Server Express 3.0 plus QFE fixes; NT 3.51 must upgrade
notes: 16-bit clients get no Y2K fixes.

metric_value: NetWare 4.11a and 3.2 are Y2K ready; older versions need downloadable upgrades from Novell website
notes: Straightforward Y2K compliance path.

metric_value: Microsoft is mindshare leader; leveraging desktop dominance to push NT Server; massive ISV/channel recruitment
notes: Aberdeen concern: hype exceeds current NT enterprise reality.

metric_value: IntranetWare provides extensive improvements to platform/print/file/application/security; good Internet integration
notes: Aberdeen concludes IntranetWare competitive with NT for enterprise.

metric_value: NOS implementations so complex they often require outside assistance; especially true for NT Server enterprise deployments

metric_value: More IT decisions based on emotional/political reasons rather than careful assessment; risks now capable of destroying companies
